version**
1.2. 
The result of `gammapy info` is below.

> System:
> 
> 	python_executable      : /Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/bin/python
> 	python_version         : 3.10.15
> 	machine                : x86_64
> 	system                 : Darwin
> 
> 
> Gammapy package:
> 
> 	version                : 1.2
> 	path                   : /Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gammapy
> 

**Bug description**
`MapDataset.fake()` does not work with HpxGeom. 

**Expected behavior**
A count map is produced with the HEALPix spatial binning.

**To Reproduce**
Please try with the following code.

```
from astropy import units as u
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord
from gammapy.maps import HpxGeom, MapAxis
from gammapy.data import Observation, FixedPointingInfo
from gammapy.irf import load_irf_dict_from_file
from gammapy.makers import MapDatasetMaker
from gammapy.datasets import MapDataset
from gammapy.modeling.models import (
    ExpCutoffPowerLawSpectralModel,
    PointSpatialModel,
    SkyModel,
    FoVBackgroundModel,
)

# Please replace the following CTA IRF path with yours.
filename = "/Users/mitsunari/Downloads/cta-prod5-zenodo-fitsonly-v0.1/fits/CTA-Performance-prod5-v0.1-North-LSTSubArray-20deg.FITS/Prod5-North-20deg-AverageAz-4LSTs.180000s-v0.1.fits"
# Define observation
pointing = SkyCoord("0d", "0d")
irfs = load_irf_dict_from_file(filename)
obs = Observation.create(
    pointing=FixedPointingInfo(fixed_icrs=pointing),
    irfs=irfs,
    livetime="1h"
    )

# Create HpxGeom
# Define energy axis. Note that the name is fixed.
energy_axis = MapAxis.from_energy_bounds(
                0.01, 100.0, 20,
                unit="TeV", name="energy"
                )
energy_true_axis = MapAxis.from_energy_bounds(
                0.005, 200, 50,
                unit="TeV", name="energy_true")

# Restrict the geometry region
hpregion_str = 'DISK({lon},{lat},{rad})'.format(
                    lon=pointing.ra.degree,
                    lat=pointing.dec.degree,
                    rad=2)
geom = HpxGeom.create(
    nside=256,
    nest=True,
    region=hpregion_str,
    skydir=pointing,
    frame="icrs",
    axes=[energy_axis]
    )
''' If I use WcsGeom instead of HpxGeom, this does not make the error.
geom = WcsGeom.create(
                    skydir=pointing,
                    binsz=0.02,
                    width=(2, 2),
                    frame="icrs",
                    proj="CAR",
                    axes=[energy_axis],
                    )
'''

maker = MapDatasetMaker(selection=["background", "edisp", "psf", "exposure"])
empty_map = MapDataset.create(
                geom=geom,
                energy_axis_true=energy_true_axis,
                name="empty"
                )
map_dataset = maker.run(empty_map, obs)

models = [FoVBackgroundModel(dataset_name="dataset-mcmc")]
models.append(
                SkyModel(
                    spatial_model=PointSpatialModel(
                        lon_0=pointing.ra,
                        lat_0=pointing.dec,
                        frame="icrs"
                        ),
                    spectral_model=ExpCutoffPowerLawSpectralModel(
                        amplitude=1e-12 * u.Unit("cm-2 s-1 TeV-1"),
                        index=2,
                        lambda_=0.1 * u.Unit("TeV-1"),
                        reference=1 * u.TeV,
                        ),
                    name="test"
                )
            )
map_dataset.models = models
map_dataset.fake()
print(map_dataset.counts)
```

 I got the following error.

> (gammapy-1.2) mitsunari@White-CTA-iMac scripts % python test_hpx_geom.py
> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/anomaly_search/pbhstudy/bh-simulator/scripts/test_hpx_geom.py", line 85, in <module>
>     map_dataset.fake()
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gammapy/datasets/map.py", line 1339, in fake
>     npred = self.npred()
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gammapy/datasets/map.py", line 636, in npred
>     npred_total = self.npred_signal()
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gammapy/datasets/map.py", line 710, in npred_signal
>     evaluator.update(
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gammapy/datasets/evaluator.py", line 219, in update
>     self.psf = psf.get_psf_kernel(
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/astropy/utils/decorators.py", line 604, in wrapper
>     return function(*args, **kwargs)
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gammapy/irf/psf/map.py", line 290, in get_psf_kernel
>     factor = _psf_upsampling_factor(self, geom, position, precision_factor)
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gammapy/irf/psf/map.py", line 27, in _psf_upsampling_factor
>     psf_r68 = psf.containment_radius(
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gammapy/irf/psf/map.py", line 219, in containment_radius
>     coords = self._get_irf_coords(**kwargs)
>   File "/Users/mitsunari/.conda/envs/gammapy-1.2/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gammapy/irf/psf/map.py", line 157, in _get_irf_coords
>     lon_pix, lat_pix = geom.coord_to_pix(coords.skycoord)
> ValueError: not enough values to unpack (expected 2, got 1)

**Other information**
I guess the cause of the error is that `HpxGeom.coord_to_pix` returns a single pixel number of HEALPix instead of the two pixel numbers of the X and Y axes.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/gammapy/gammapy/issues/5505/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/gammapy/gammapy/issues/5505/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

**Expected behavior**
A count map is produced with the HEALPix spatial binning.

**To Reproduce**
Please try with the following code.

```

from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord

    ExpCutoffPowerLawSpectralModel,
    PointSpatialModel,
    SkyModel,
    FoVBackgroundModel,

# Please replace the following CTA IRF path with yours.
filename = "/Users/mitsunari/Downloads/cta-prod5-zenodo-fitsonly-v0.1/fits/CTA-Performance-prod5-v0.1-North-LSTSubArray-20deg.FITS/Prod5-North-20deg-AverageAz-4LSTs.180000s-v0.1.fits"
# Define observation
pointing = SkyCoord("0d", "0d")
irfs = load_irf_dict_from_file(filename)
obs = Observation.create(
    pointing=FixedPointingInfo(fixed_icrs=pointing),
    irfs=irfs,
    livetime="1h"
    )

# Create HpxGeom
# Define energy axis. Note that the name is fixed.
energy_axis = MapAxis.from_energy_bounds(
                0.01, 100.0, 20,
                unit="TeV", name="energy"
                )
energy_true_axis = MapAxis.from_energy_bounds(
                0.005, 200, 50,
                unit="TeV", name="energy_true")

# Restrict the geometry region
hpregion_str = 'DISK({lon},{lat},{rad})'.format(
                    lon=pointing.ra.degree,
                    lat=pointing.dec.degree,
                    rad=2)
geom = HpxGeom.create(
    nside=256,
    nest=True,
    region=hpregion_str,

    frame="icrs",
    axes=[energy_axis]
    )
''' If I use WcsGeom instead of HpxGeom, this does not make the error.
geom = WcsGeom.create(
                    skydir=pointing,
                    binsz=0.02,
                    width=(2, 2),
                    frame="icrs",
                    proj="CAR",
                    axes=[energy_axis],
                    )
'''

maker = MapDatasetMaker(selection=["background", "edisp", "psf", "exposure"])
empty_map = MapDataset.create(
                geom=geom,
                energy_axis_true=energy_true_axis,
                name="empty"
                )
map_dataset = maker.run(empty_map, obs)

models = [FoVBackgroundModel(dataset_name="dataset-mcmc")]
models.append(
                SkyModel(
                    spatial_model=PointSpatialModel(

                        ),
                    spectral_model=ExpCutoffPowerLawSpectralModel(

                        index=2,
                        lambda_=0.1 * u.Unit("TeV-1"),
                        reference=1 * u.TeV,
                        ),
                    name="test"
                )
            )

**Bug description**
`MapDataset.fake()` does not work with HpxGeom. 

**Expected behavior**
A count map is produced with the HEALPix spatial binning.

**To Reproduce**
Please try with the following code.

```
from astropy import units as u
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord
from gammapy.maps import HpxGeom, MapAxis
from gammapy.data import Observation, FixedPointingInfo
from gammapy.irf import load_irf_dict_from_file
from gammapy.makers import MapDatasetMaker
from gammapy.datasets import MapDataset
from gammapy.modeling.models import (
    ExpCutoffPowerLawSpectralModel,
    PointSpatialModel,
    SkyModel,
    FoVBackgroundModel,
)

# Please replace the following CTA IRF path with yours.
filename = "/Users/mitsunari/Downloads/cta-prod5-zenodo-fitsonly-v0.1/fits/CTA-Performance-prod5-v0.1-North-LSTSubArray-20deg.FITS/Prod5-North-20deg-AverageAz-4LSTs.180000s-v0.1.fits"
# Define observation
pointing = SkyCoord("0d", "0d")
irfs = load_irf_dict_from_file(filename)
obs = Observation.create(
    pointing=FixedPointingInfo(fixed_icrs=pointing),
    irfs=irfs,
    livetime="1h"
    )

# Create HpxGeom
# Define energy axis. Note that the name is fixed.
energy_axis = MapAxis.from_energy_bounds(
                0.01, 100.0, 20,
                unit="TeV", name="energy"
                )
energy_true_axis = MapAxis.from_energy_bounds(
                0.005, 200, 50,
                unit="TeV", name="energy_true")

# Restrict the geometry region
hpregion_str = 'DISK({lon},{lat},{rad})'.format(
                    lon=pointing.ra.degree,
                    lat=pointing.dec.degree,
                    rad=2)
geom = HpxGeom.create(
    nside=256,
    nest=True,
    region=hpregion_str,
    skydir=pointing,
    frame="icrs",
    axes=[energy_axis]
    )
''' If I use WcsGeom instead of HpxGeom, this does not make the error.
geom = WcsGeom.create(
                    skydir=pointing,
                    binsz=0.02,
                    width=(2, 2),
                    frame="icrs",
                    proj="CAR",
                    axes=[energy_axis],
                    )
'''

maker = MapDatasetMaker(selection=["background", "edisp", "psf", "exposure"])
empty_map = MapDataset.create(
                geom=geom,
                energy_axis_true=energy_true_axis,
                name="empty"
                )
map_dataset = maker.run(empty_map, obs)

models = [FoVBackgroundModel(dataset_name="dataset-mcmc")]
models.append(
                SkyModel(
                    spatial_model=PointSpatialModel(
                        lon_0=pointing.ra,
                        lat_0=pointing.dec,
                        frame="icrs"
                        ),
                    spectral_model=ExpCutoffPowerLawSpectralModel(
                        amplitude=1e-12 * u.Unit("cm-2 s-1 TeV-1"),
                        index=2,
                        lambda_=0.1 * u.Unit("TeV-1"),
                        reference=1 * u.TeV,
                        ),
                    name="test"
                )
            )
map_dataset.models = models
map_dataset.fake()
print(map_dataset.counts)
```

 I got the following error.

    ExpCutoffPowerLawSpectralModel,

)

# Please replace the following CTA IRF path with yours.
filename = "/Users/mitsunari/Downloads/cta-prod5-zenodo-fitsonly-v0.1/fits/CTA-Performance-prod5-v0.1-North-LSTSubArray-20deg.FITS/Prod5-North-20deg-AverageAz-4LSTs.180000s-v0.1.fits"

obs = Observation.create(
    pointing=FixedPointingInfo(fixed_icrs=pointing),
    irfs=irfs,
    livetime="1h"
    )

# Create HpxGeom

energy_axis = MapAxis.from_energy_bounds(
                0.01, 100.0, 20,
                unit="TeV", name="energy"
                )
energy_true_axis = MapAxis.from_energy_bounds(

geom = HpxGeom.create(
